meta.title: Legal & Other-Requirements Compliance Register
meta.subtitle: Multi-jurisdiction register (UK + India) — Northgate Manufacturing, Plant 2
meta.author: HSE Operations
meta.deid_notice: All compliance owners are referred to by role label. Identifiers were pseudonymised before drafting; the re-identification key is held separately and is not part of this document.

sections.text: This legal & other-requirements compliance register baselines the applicable obligations for Northgate Manufacturing, Plant 2, across two jurisdictions (UK + India/Maharashtra) for the activities the site performs: general workplace, manual handling, and solvent degreasing. It is built per ISO 45001 clause 6.1.3 (determination of legal and other requirements) and evaluates compliance per clause 9.1.2. Each obligation is confirmed applicable to a named activity, mapped to its compliance evidence, and tracked to a role-label owner and a review date. The India leg defers to the hse-india engine after mandatory state detection; no national form number is minted.

rows: General duty of care to employees
Control of substances hazardous to health
sections.caption: UK obligations — applicability confirmed per activity; LEV test record is a [GAP-evidence]

rows: Factories Act 1948 + Maharashtra Factory Rules 1963 (resolve via hse-india)
Maharashtra Factory Rules — state form [GAP] (run hse-india: factories-act-returns)
Maharashtra Factory Rules — state form [GAP] (run hse-india: india-state-form-finder)
OSH Code 2020 (KB-REG-IN-OSH-CODE) — commencement pending in MH
sections.caption: India obligations route to hse-india after state detection (Maharashtra); state-specific form numbers shown as [GAP] — never minted; legacy Maharashtra Factory Rules are the primary answer with the OSH-Code direction flagged

rows.title: LEV thorough-examination record missing
rows.description: COSHH-required local exhaust ventilation on the degreasing line has no current thorough-examination certificate on file ([GAP-evidence]).

rows.title: OSH Code 2020 transition unmonitored
rows.description: Maharashtra commencement of the OSH Code is pending; the legacy Factory Rules filings remain valid, but no transition watch is assigned.

rows.title: Inapplicable construction reg excluded
rows.description: CDM 2015 was screened and excluded — Plant 2 performs no construction activity; recorded as a confirmed-inapplicable exclusion, not a listed obligation.

rows.action: Schedule and record the LEV thorough examination on the degreasing line
Assign and run an OSH-Code transition watch for Maharashtra (defer detail to hse-india)

sections.text: India deferral note — state detected: MAHARASHTRA. State-specific form numbers (annual return, accident notice) are resolved by the hse-india engine (run india-state-form-finder / factories-act-returns) and are shown here as [GAP] pending that resolution. No national form number has been minted. If hse-india is not installed, run those skills for Maharashtra to complete the India rows.

This register is decision-support and must be reviewed by a competent person before reliance. Each obligation's review date drives re-verification; a legal change — or commencement of the OSH Code in Maharashtra — triggers an out-of-cycle review. Compliance owners are role-labelled; the re-identification key is held separately.
